projectDescription: A C++ command-line tool that minimizes Boolean functions using the Quine–McCluskey algorithm, producing a minimal sum-of-products expression from a truth table input.
keyFeatures: Minimizes Boolean functions from a truth table output string
Implements the Quine–McCluskey (QMA) algorithm for exact minimization
Groups minterms iteratively by number of 1s and pairs close minterms
Builds an Essential Prime Implicants (EPI) table to find a minimal cover
Falls back to brute-force power-set search for optimal non-essential coverage
Outputs a minimal sum-of-products Boolean expression
Prints intermediate truth table, minterm groups, and EPI table for debugging
publicApiSurface: Minimizer(string in) — constructor accepting truth table output as a binary string
string Minimizer::minimize() — runs the full QMA algorithm and returns the minimized Boolean equation
void Minimizer::printTruthTable() — prints the full truth table to stdout
void Minimizer::printMintermGroups() — prints current minterm groups to stdout
void Minimizer::printEPITable() — prints the Essential Prime Implicants table to stdout
Minterm(int mv, bool dc, string ab) — construct a single minterm
Minterm(const Minterm& m1, const Minterm& m2, string ab) — construct a combined minterm
string Minterm::getAsBinary() const — returns binary representation
string Minterm::getAsLogic() const — returns logic expression (e.g. A!BC)
vector<int> Minterm::getMintermValues() const — returns covered minterm indices
bool Minterm::contains(int elem) const — checks if a minterm covers a given index
bool Minterm::isDontCare() const — checks if minterm is a don't-care
CLI: ./minimize [n-bit string] — minimize from command line where n is a power of 2
